name: Marble Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: Vegetable oil (3 tbsp), preferably canola oil
Unsweetened cocoa powder (2 1/2 tbsp), Dutch-process or American-style
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Coat a 12-cup tube pan with nonstick cooking spray. If the pan does not have a removable bottom, insert a ring of wax paper cut to fit the pan bottom. Dust the pan with flour, tapping out excess.
3. In a small heavy saucepan, melt butter over medium heat; cook gently until the butter is just nutty brown, about 3 to 5 minutes. Remove from burner and let cool slightly.
4. Add oil to the melted butter in a small metal bowl. Freeze the butter-oil mixture until firm but not hard, about 15 minutes.
5. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt onto wax paper into a medium-sized bowl.
6. In a large mixing bowl, combine the butter-oil mixture, sugar, whole egg, and egg whites; beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until very light and well blended, about 4 minutes. Blend in vanilla extract and almond extract.
7. With the mixer on low speed, beat in half of the dry ingredients just until incorporated.
8. Beat in yogurt and milk, then remaining dry ingredients just until smoothly incorporated; do not overbeat.
9. Place cocoa powder in a medium-sized bowl. Pour 3 tbsp very hot water over cocoa, stirring until completely smooth.
10. Measure out 1-1/4 cups cake batter. Slowly stir the butter into the pan.
11. Spoon the cocoa batter over vanilla batter, forming pools on the surface. Top with pools of remaining vanilla batter.
12. Run a table knife around the pan sides and center tube to loosen the cake. Tap the pan sharply on the counter several times to eliminate air pockets produced by marbling.
13. Bake for 50 to 65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the thickest part comes out clean and the top springs back when lightly pressed. Transfer the pan to a wire rack and let stand until the cake is cool.
14. Run a knife around the pan sides and center tube to loosen the cake. Transfer to a cake plate.
15. Serves 16. The "sour cream" in this recipe is actually nonfat yogurt, which adds moisture without fat.
